summary: Continual-learning platform that turns product usage into better AI behavior.
editor_note: The sharp angle is post-training from live product usage, not another eval dashboard. Trajectory's 4-step platform instruments customer workflows, captures traces and corrections, and trains improvements back into prompts, harnesses, and models. Unlike static fine-tuning projects, it is framed around Clay, Decagon, and Harvey use cases. The risk is whether continual learning becomes infrastructure or remains bespoke research service work.

product_evidence.claim: The official site says Trajectory turns real product usage into AI that continuously improves.
The platform section describes a lightweight SDK that captures traces, corrections, re-prompts, and edits, then helps teams observe, steer, learn, and deploy.

market_context.primary_user: AI product teams that need model behavior to improve from live customer workflows.
market_context.differentiation: Feedback from actual product usage becomes a continual-learning loop rather than a static evaluation report.
market_context.why_now: The 2026 seed round backs infrastructure for AI teams moving from launch-time prompting into ongoing behavior improvement.

risks.claim: The open question is whether continual-learning workflows become standard product infrastructure or stay tied to bespoke AI research engagements.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
